cure_wedge: durable MRD-negative remission and relapse prevention

scoring_rubric.scale: 0 to 5 per dimension; higher means stronger public-research priority, not clinical superiority
scoring_rubric.score_total_interpretation: Used only to order public tooling and review work; not a treatment, trial, or investment recommendation.

opportunities.title: Map relapse mechanisms after CAR T-cell therapy
opportunities.question: When myeloma relapses after deep CAR T response, how often do public sources implicate target loss, T-cell fitness, tumor burden, disease location, or prior exposure?
opportunities.why_it_matters: Durability depends on understanding why deep responses fail.
opportunities.artifact_next_step: Create a public mechanism table separating antigen escape, immune exhaustion, disease burden, and site-specific relapse hypotheses.
limitations: Mechanism frequency cannot be inferred from source summaries alone.
Product-specific and disease-state-specific evidence must stay separated.

opportunities.title: Map MRD-guided durability evidence
opportunities.question: Which public sources connect measurable residual disease negativity, sustained response, relapse timing, and survival endpoints in defined myeloma settings?
opportunities.why_it_matters: Cure-oriented work needs measurement anchors before it can compare interventions.
opportunities.artifact_next_step: Build an MRD endpoint glossary and claim set with source-backed definitions and uncertainty.
limitations: MRD methods, thresholds, timing, and sample context are not interchangeable.
Endpoint association is not the same as intervention causality.

opportunities.title: Track dual-antigen and sequential immune-redirection strategies
opportunities.question: Which trials and papers test whether targeting more than one myeloma-associated antigen can reduce escape after BCMA-directed therapy?
opportunities.why_it_matters: Antigen escape is a plausible relapse pathway after single-target immune pressure.
opportunities.artifact_next_step: Create a trial landscape for BCMA, GPRC5D, FcRH5, and other immune-redirection target combinations.
limitations: Trial listings do not prove efficacy or availability.
Target combinations may carry different toxicity and eligibility boundaries.

opportunities.title: Map immune reconstitution and infection-risk constraints
opportunities.question: How do immune recovery, infection risk, and supportive care shape the feasibility of deep-response strategies?
opportunities.why_it_matters: Durability-focused therapy is only useful if patients can safely receive and recover from it.
opportunities.artifact_next_step: Create a source-backed supportive-care constraint map for immune therapies and intensive strategies.
limitations: Public summaries are not enough to make clinical safety decisions.

opportunities.title: Track vaccine and immune-priming research for relapse prevention
opportunities.question: Where do public trials or papers explore vaccine, immune-priming, or immunoprevention strategies for precursor states or post-response relapse prevention?
opportunities.why_it_matters: A cure/vaccine initiative needs a careful lane for immunoprevention without overstating maturity.
opportunities.artifact_next_step: Build a trial and literature map that separates precursor-state prevention from post-treatment relapse-prevention hypotheses.
limitations: Does not imply an established myeloma vaccine or prevention strategy.
Requires careful separation of precursor disease, remission, and relapsed disease settings.

review_notes: Initial opportunity scores are a prioritization scaffold for public tooling work, not clinical rankings.
Scores should be revised after expert review and source-specific extraction.
